lede: A look at December 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Tom Cannon (footballer) and Elizabeth Jordan Carr, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Indonesia AirAsia Flight 8501
events.desc: Indonesia AirAsia Flight 8501 crashes into the Karimata Strait en route from Surabaya to Singapore, killing all 162 people aboard.

events.title: MS Norman Atlantic
events.desc: Nine people die and another 19 are reported missing, when the MS Norman Atlantic catches fire in the Strait of Otranto, in the Adriatic Sea, in Italian waters.

events.title: 2009 Karachi bombing
events.desc: Forty-three people die in a suicide bombing in Karachi, Pakistan, where Shia Muslims are observing the Day of Ashura.

events.title: War in Somalia (2006–2009)
events.desc: War in Somalia: The militaries of Somalia's Transitional Federal Government and Ethiopian troops capture Mogadishu unopposed.

events.title: Muriel Siebert
events.desc: American businesswoman Muriel Siebert becomes the first woman to own a seat on the New York Stock Exchange.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Anthropoid, the plot to assassinate high-ranking Nazi officer Reinhard Heydrich, commences.

events.title: 1908 Messina earthquake
events.desc: The 7.1 Mw Messina earthquake shakes Southern Italy with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XI (Extreme), killing between about 80,000.

events.title: Taksin
events.desc: King Taksin's coronation achieved through conquest as a king of Thailand and established Thonburi as a capital.

events.title: 893 Dvin earthquake
events.desc: An earthquake destroys the city of Dvin, Armenia.

events.title: Alaric II
events.desc: Alaric II succeeds his father Euric and becomes king of the Visigoths. He establishes his capital at Aire-sur-l'Adour (Southern Gaul).

deaths.text: Brigitte Bardot, French actress, singer and activist
Max Steiner, Austrian-American pianist, composer, and conductor

observances.text: Christian feast day: Feast of the Holy Innocents or Childermas; in Spain and Latin American countries the festival is celebrated with pranks (inocentadas), similar to April Fools' Day (Catholic Church, Church of England, Lutheran Church), and its related observances: Els Enfarinats (Ibi, Spain)
Proclamation Day (South Australia), celebration started on the day following Christmas (South Australia)
